text.title: AI Story Truth Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every AI-generated story MUST pass. Hard rules; "the AI seems confident" is not a valid reason to skip them.

rule.statement: The story's primary persona ("As a <persona>...") MUST appear verbatim in `personas.yaml`. Synonyms, role-noun substitutions ("user", "manager") that do not match a named persona are REJECTED. Fabricated personas trigger `scrap`.
rule.rationale: LLMs invent personas under prompt pressure ("write a story for the busy enterprise admin"). The persona roster is the source of truth; new personas require user research, not a story-generation prompt.

rule.statement: Every story MUST reference a specific JTBD statement from `research/jtbd.md` in a `jtbd_ref` field. Stories without a JTBD anchor are REJECTED. The BA verifies that the story's "I want" clause maps to the JTBD's progress motion (not a solution).
rule.rationale: Solution-first stories ("As X I want to click button Y") bypass the job. JTBD anchoring forces the story to express progress, which is what the team should build for.

rule.statement: Any numeric claim in the story narrative ("60% of users", "saves 4 hours/week") MUST trace to a research artefact via inline citation. Fabricated stats — common in LLM output — trigger `revise` AND removal of the stat. The narrative may stand without the stat, never with a hallucinated one.
rule.rationale: LLMs fabricate plausible-sounding metrics. Citation-tracing is the cheapest defence; the narrative without a stat is usually clearer than with a wrong one.

rule.statement: Every story MUST include at least 3 edge cases in the AC: a failure path (e.g. API down), an empty / zero state (e.g. no data), and a privacy / consent implication where applicable. Stories that ship with only happy-path AC are REJECTED.

rationale.source: QA shift-left practice; LLM happy-path bias

rule.rationale: LLMs draft happy-path AC by default. Enumerating the three edge cases at story-time prevents PR-time scrambling and forces the team to discuss failure shapes early.

rule.statement: Every acceptance criterion MUST be testable AND traceable to a specific behaviour. AC that read "the system works as expected" or "the UI is intuitive" are REJECTED. Each AC uses Given/When/Then OR an equivalent BDD-style with explicit observable outcome.
rule.rationale: Untestable AC create no closure signal. Given/When/Then forces concrete behaviour, which the QA can verify and the developer can implement against. Vague AC are LLM-comfort filler.

section.title: The 8-point checklist (summary)

list.item: 6. Voice / tense correct: "As a X, I want Y, so that Z" — no role-noun "the user".
7. Dependencies named (not "depends on the system") — link by ticket / module.
8. Out-of-scope items moved to a separate "not in this story" note.

section.reference: The 8-point checklist with concrete prompts and examples per check.

example.title: Invented persona + fabricated stat + happy-path AC
example.code:
As a busy enterprise admin I want to bulk-import 1000 users at once So that I save 4 hours per week (60% of admins requested this).
AC: - The system imports users. - The UI is intuitive.
example.note: r1, r3, r4, r5 violations. "busy enterprise admin" not in roster. Stat fabricated. AC vague and happy-path only. Decision: scrap.

example.title: Properly-vetted story
example.code:
As a Site Administrator (persona ref: persona-admin-01) I want to bulk-import users from a CSV file So that I onboard a cohort without per-user clicks (JTBD ref: jtbd-onboard-cohort).
AC: - Given a CSV with valid rows, when I upload, then all rows are imported. - Given a CSV with malformed rows, when I upload, then valid rows import and invalid rows produce a per-row error report. - Given a CSV with > 5000 rows, when I upload, then the system processes in chunks and reports progress. - Given a CSV with email addresses already in the system, when I upload, then duplicates are skipped without error. - Edge: when the CSV upload API is unavailable, the user sees a retry prompt with cause. Privacy: confirm consent flag check per row (per data-policy doc 2026-04).
example.note: r1, r2, r4, r5 satisfied. Persona ref + JTBD ref present. AC are Given/When/Then. Edge cases enumerated. Privacy implication acknowledged.
